verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Rey Cortiço Mid-morning — 11:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço looks like the best bodyboard option for mid-morning — 11:00.  This should feel like a fun progression session rather than a heavy one.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: Rey Cortiço looks like a rare, worth-it window for TODAY—mid-morning around 11:00 is the sweet spot. Wind looks good and glassy, and while it’s running dead low, it can still line up nicely on the Belgas-style sandbanks if they’re set. I’d check the Surfline and MEO Beachcam first, but with the consistency coming through, I’d go.

best.windFit: SSW 5 km/h · glassy · good wind fit

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 71 kJ, 8 s period, workable consistency 59/100, and glassy conditions.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at low tide can still work on Belgas if the sandbars are lined up, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: scan from the dunes first because banks and channels shift fast.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 60 kJ, 8 s period, lully 29/100, and glassy conditions.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a clean fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 90 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 74/100, and glassy conditions.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Expect: it is less reliable than the main bay spots, so weak swell or short period can make it feel soft.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a clean fun-sized window for Fun progression, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 74 kJ, 8 s period, workable consistency 57/100, and glassy conditions.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves.  Watch: 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.
warnings: 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves
